proposal.thesis: Model fair probability shows 52.2% chance of BTC being below $63,556 by July 2026, giving a meaningful 4.4 percentage point edge over the 50-50 breakeven. With 18+ months to expiry and strike near current levels, betting the more probable outcome offers positive expected value worth maximum position size.

review.verdict: VETO. Historical calibration shows models in the 0.40-0.50 bucket have catastrophic performance: actual outcomes occur only 26.5% vs implied 46.9%, producing -41.6% ROI. The Strategist's 52.2% fair probability falls in this systematically overconfident range where the model has lost money consistently. The claimed 4.4pp edge evaporates entirely when calibration adjustment is applied, making this a negative expected value bet at any size.

review.verdict: VETO. The model shows fair P(up)=0.478, placing this in the 0.40-0.50 bucket where historical calibration reveals systematic over-optimism: actual outcomes show only 26.9% up vs implied 46.9%, a catastrophic -20pp gap with -40.9% ROI. Calibration-adjusted probability suggests true P(up)≈0.67, completely reversing the supposed edge. This bet would be placing maximum capital on a position where historical data strongly indicates we have the direction wrong.
